subHeadline: A permanent, categorised reference — not symptom-chasing blog posts. Every entry links to a full breakdown of symptoms, root cause, mileage window, repair cost, and replacement cost.

rows.title: OM651 "Black Death" Injector Seal Failure
rows.description: The defining Mercedes diesel issue and our #1 enquiry driver. Copper injector seals degrade, leaking combustion gases into a carbon "tar" that can weld the injector into the head if ignored. 1,250 enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED]. £400–£1,200 seal repair; up to full engine replacement if neglected [MB-QUOTE].

rows.title: OM651 Rear Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
rows.description: Unlike most engines, the OM651's chain sits at the transmission end — access requires separating the engine from the gearbox, making an otherwise routine job labour-intensive.

rows.title: OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Leak
rows.description: The V6 diesel's primary weak point — an O-ring fails in the "V" of the engine, requiring significant disassembly to access. 880 enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED]. £800–£1,500 repair [MB-QUOTE].

rows.title: M276 Timing Chain Stretch
rows.description: Chain stretch on earlier examples, plus carbon buildup on intake valves — well-understood and rarely catastrophic if caught early. 390 enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

rows.title: M177 Valve Cover Oil Leaks
rows.description: The AMG V8's most common complaint — generally robust otherwise. 370 enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED]. £800–£1,500 repair [MB-QUOTE].

rows.title: Chemical Smell & Black Tar Around Injectors
rows.description: The unmistakable sign of "Black Death" — remove the engine cover and inspect at the first hint of a chemical smell.

rows.title: Hissing or Chuffing Noise Under Acceleration
rows.description: Often an early-stage injector seal leak before visible carbon buildup appears.

rows.title: AdBlue System Warnings
rows.description: OM654-specific — usually a NOx sensor or heater fault, software-manageable rather than mechanical.

rows.title: Coolant Loss With No Visible Leak
rows.description: OM642 oil cooler seal or M276 gasket weep — investigate before it leads to overheating.
